buggy_code:
def compute(self, today, assets, out, returns, *inputs):
    self._maybe_train_model(today, returns, inputs)
    X = np.dstack(inputs)[-1]
    X = self._scaler.transform(X)
    out[:] = self._model.predict(X)
description: Model training is triggered inside compute(), which is called on every pipeline execution.
explanation: compute() is called frequently during a backtest. Training inside it means the model is re-fitted on different data windows at different times depending on execution order, causing non-deterministic behavior. It also means training uses whatever data happens to be passed in at that moment, which may include current-day data that would not be available in live trading.
suggested_fix: Separate training from inference. Train the model in a scheduled function before_trading_start or before the backtest loop begins. compute() should only do inference.
